mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or out-of-sample classification accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which jointly drive corporate revenue growth and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generate positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of rising inflation expectations, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.3% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or PAYEMS and UNRATE diverge without affecting market direction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets price in macroeconomic strength.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a contraction in manufacturing activity that immediately reduces earnings forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls > 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods in walk-forward validation.
